sections.paragraph:
-

(1) i.q. Arab. عَادَ Med. Waw, to turn back, to return (the verbs אוּד No. 1, and תוּד see Piel, are nearly connected); then to repeat, to do over again (compare שׁוּב); whence inf. absol. עֹוד adv. again, yet (which see).

(2) to say again and again, to witness, to exhort, in Kal once, Lam 2:13 כתיב, see Hiphil.

Piel עִוֵּד to surround, Psa 119:61. (Eth. ዐዊድ፡ to go round, አዖ፡ to make go round, i.e. to surround.)

Hiphil הִעֵיד.-

(1) causat. to take as a witness, to call any one to witness, Isa 8:2 Jer 32:10, 25 Jer 32:25, 44 Jer 32:44hence to call as witness, to invoke, followed by בְּ against any one, Deu 4:26 30:19 31:28 .

(2) i.q. Kal; hence to testify, to bear witness, absol. Amo 3:13 Mal 2:14, followed by an acc. against any one, 1Ki 21:10, 13 1Ki 21:13, and in a good sense for any one, i.e, to praise him, Job 29:11 (compare μαρτυρέω, Luk 4:22). Hence-(a) to obtest, i.e. to affirm solemnly, to affirm, calling God to witness, followed by בְּ of pers. Gen 43:3, הָעֵד הֵעִד בָּנוּ הָאִישׁ לֵּאמֹר “the man did solemnly affirm unto us;” Deu 8:19 32:46 1Ki 2:42 Zec 3:6.-(b) to admonish solemnly, especially Jehovah a people, followed by an acc. of pers. Lam 2:13 בְּ Psa 50:7 81:9 2Ki 17:13 עַלּ Jer 6:10 especially to chastise, to chide (compare יָסַר), Neh 13:15, 21 Neh 13:21.-(c) solemnly to enjoin on any one a precept or law; hence used of any law given by God (see עֵדָה No. 3 , and עֵדוּת ), 2Ki 17:15, אֵת עֵדֹותָיו אֲשֶׁר הֵעִיד בָּם “his precepts, which he had given them;” Neh 9:34 1Sa 8:9.

Hophal הוּעֵד to be declared, shewn, Exo 21:29.

Pilel עֹדֵד (which some incorrectly take as from the root עָדַד) pr. to restore, to confirm, Psa 146:9 147:6 .

Hithpalel, to set oneself up, to stand upright, Psa 20:9. LXX. ἀνορθώθημεν.

Derived nouns, עֵד, עֵדָה, עֵדוּת, תְּעוּדָה [pr.n. עֹודֵד] and-
